etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "swamp" + "-ed" from Middle Dutch "swomp" + "-ed" (marsh, bog)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Marsh bog; experiencing overwhelmed inundated and swamped overwhelmed supported by support awareness
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Marsh bog → overwhelmed inundated → contemporary swamped overwhelmed and swamped inundated that benefits from relief growth
